principle: Classifies every legacy audit-witness + report type against the canonical MPR taxonomy. KEEP = canonical (a pillar witness, the composite/fractal rollup, or an AaaS-lane artifact). ARCHIVE = drift/superseded/dated-one-off → moved to audit/archive/legacy-scrub-2026-06-28/ per repo (recoverable). Staleness is refreshed by cadence, NOT scrubbed.

superseded-five-core.reason: Five-core (A1–A5) model superseded by the 11-pillar MPR taxonomy; content maps to pillar witnesses.

forensic-scratch.reason: Per-run forensic scratch; not a standing audit witness.

docs-hygiene-fragments.reason: Fold into worldClass.docs-hygiene + the closed-specs provisioning gate; many overlapping fragments.

legacy-reports.reason: One-off/dated narrative reports superseded by the canonical tier reports + mpr-scorecard + remediation-roadmap.

execution.perRepo: move ARCHIVE-class witnesses (audit/evidence/<type>-latest.json + dated siblings) and reports into audit/archive/legacy-scrub-2026-06-28/, commit (batched under gates), do not push until reviewed.
execution.rebuild: after scrub, run aaas:registry:build + aaas:contract:check to confirm the fleet reduces to the canonical set.
